column3: Given
column4: When
column5: Then

column3: autoFetchDeviceToken: true (default) and APNPushProvider is configured
column4: configure() completes
column5: SDK calls UIApplication.shared.registerForRemoteNotifications() automatically

column3: autoFetchDeviceToken: false
column4: configure() completes
column5: SDK does not call registerForRemoteNotifications(); the host app must call it manually

column3: APNPushProvider is configured
column4: didRegisterForRemoteNotifications(withDeviceToken:) is called
column5: The token is persisted to DeviceStore and a Device Created or Updated event is uploaded

column3: A custom PushTokenProvider (FCM wrapper) is configured
column4: observeTokenRefresh callback fires with an FCM token
column5: The token is persisted to DeviceStore and a Device Created or Updated event is uploaded

column3: A token is already registered
column4: didRegisterForRemoteNotifications fires with the same token
column5: No Device Created or Updated event is uploaded; the token is already current

column3: A token is already registered
column4: didRegisterForRemoteNotifications fires with a new token
column5: The new token is persisted and a Device Created or Updated event is uploaded

column3: tokenFromAPNSData returns nil (FCM token not yet ready)
column4: didRegisterForRemoteNotifications is called
column5: No device update is sent immediately; the token arrives later via the observeTokenRefresh callback

column3: A device token is registered
column4: unregisterDevice() is called
column5: A Device Deleted event is sent to the backend; the local token is retained in DeviceStore

column3: No token has been registered
column4: unregisterDevice() is called
column5: No event is sent; the call returns silently

column3: A device token is registered and a user is identified
column4: identify() is called with a different user ID
column5: A Device Deleted event is sent for the previous profile then identify fires then Device Created or Updated re-registers the token to the new profile

column3: A device token is registered and no user is identified
column4: identify("user-123") is called
column5: Device Deleted fires then the identify event fires then Device Created or Updated re-registers the token to the new profile

column3: showInForeground: true (default) and autoTrackPushEvents: true
column4: A CIO alert push arrives while the app is foregrounded
column5: The push banner is shown and a delivered metric is tracked

column3: showInForeground: false and autoTrackPushEvents: true
column4: A CIO alert push arrives while the app is foregrounded
column5: The push banner is suppressed; a delivered metric is still tracked

column3: autoTrackPushEvents: true
column4: A non-CIO alert push arrives while the app is foregrounded
column5: The push is forwarded to the proxy; no CIO metric is tracked

column3: autoTrackPushEvents: true
column4: The user taps a CIO push notification
column5: An opened metric is tracked and any deep link in the payload is routed

column3: autoTrackPushEvents: true
column4: The user dismisses a CIO push notification without tapping
column5: No opened metric is tracked

column3: A push contains a deep link URL and an explicit deepLinkCallback is registered
column4: The user taps the push
column5: The registered callback is invoked with the URL; the system open path is not used

column3: A push contains an http/https deep link and no callback is registered
column4: The user taps the push
column5: UIApplication.shared.continue(NSUserActivity) is called to trigger universal link handling

column3: A push contains a custom-scheme deep link and no callback is registered
column4: The user taps the push
column5: UIApplication.shared.open(url) is called to route the custom scheme

column3: A push contains no deep link field
column4: The user taps the push
column5: No URL routing occurs; only the opened metric is tracked

column3: NSE is present and push has mutable-content: 1
column4: Push arrives while app is backgrounded
column5: The NSE fires and records a delivered metric; the App Group file is written before the upload attempt

column3: NSE is present and push has mutable-content: 1
column4: Push arrives while app is force-quit
column5: The NSE fires and records a delivered metric independent of the main app state

column3: NSE is present and push has mutable-content: 1 and app is foregrounded
column4: Push arrives
column5: NSE records delivered; willPresent also calls trackDelivered; backend deduplicates to one metric

column3: No NSE target is configured
column4: Push arrives while app is backgrounded
column5: Delivery is not tracked; no callbacks fire until the user taps

column3: No NSE target is configured
column4: Push arrives while app is foregrounded
column5: willPresent fires and a delivered metric is tracked

column3: content-available: 1 push (silent push) arrives
column4: Any app state
column5: The SDK performs no handling; the host app's didReceiveRemoteNotification:fetchCompletionHandler: fires

column3: App Group is configured
column4: The NSE processes a CIO push
column5: A delivery record is written atomically: first to delivery-UUID.tmp then renamed to delivery-UUID.json

column3: A delivery-UUID.json record exists in the App Group queue
column4: The main app calls configure()
column5: The record is decoded enqueued as a Report Delivery Event and the file is deleted immediately after enqueue

column3: The extension is killed after writing delivery-UUID.tmp but before the rename
column4: The main app calls configure()
column5: The .tmp straggler is deleted and no delivery event is generated for it

column3: Multiple delivery records are in the App Group queue
column4: The main app calls configure()
column5: Each record is decoded enqueued and deleted one at a time; a mid-drain crash does not orphan already-deleted records

column3: No App Group is configured
column4: The NSE processes a CIO push
column5: A background URLSession upload task is created; the OS continues the upload after the extension process exits

column3: reset() is called
column4: The App Group push-delivery directory exists
column5: The entire io.customer.sdk/push-delivery/ directory is deleted

column3: A CIO push payload contains a cioImage.url
column4: The NSE fires
column5: The image is downloaded and attached as a UNNotificationAttachment; the notification appears with the image

column3: A CIO push payload contains a cioImage.url
column4: The image download fails or times out
column5: The NSE calls the content handler with the unmodified text content; the notification still appears

column3: serviceExtensionTimeWillExpire fires
column4: An image download is in progress
column5: The download is cancelled and the content handler is called immediately with the best content assembled so far

column3: startConfigure is used and a push click arrives before configure() completes
column4: Configure finishes
column5: The buffered click response is processed in order with its completion handler called; the opened metric is tracked

column3: configure() is used without activateModulesForLaunch and a push click arrives before configure() completes
column4: The push is delivered at cold launch
column5: The buffering registrar is not installed; the click may be lost

column3: A CIO push triggers both NSE delivery and willPresent delivery in the same session
column4: Both events arrive for the same pushId and deliveryDate
column5: PushHistory records the first event as handled; the second is a no-op and only one delivered metric reaches the backend

column3: A 3rd-party SDK forwards a push event back to the CIO SDK after CIO has already handled it
column4: The event arrives for the same pushId and deliveryDate
column5: PushHistory exits early on the second arrival; no duplicate metric is tracked

column3: A push payload contains aps.event (a Live Activity push with an alert banner)
column4: The user taps the banner (didReceive fires)
column5: The SDK exits immediately without tracking an opened metric routing a deep link or forwarding to the proxy

column3: A push payload contains aps.event
column4: willPresent fires for the alert banner
column5: The SDK exits immediately without tracking a delivered metric; the completion handler is called with an empty presentation options array

column3: A push payload does not contain CIO-Delivery-ID
column4: didReceive fires
column5: The push is forwarded to PushEventHandlerProxy but no CIO metric is tracked

column3: autoTrackPushEvents: false
column4: configure() completes
column5: The SDK does not register as UNUserNotificationCenter delegate; the host app must manually forward push events

column3: autoTrackPushEvents: false and userNotificationCenter(didReceive:withCompletionHandler:) is forwarded manually
column4: A CIO push is tapped
column5: The SDK handles the CIO push (tracks opened metric routes deep link) and returns true

column3: autoTrackPushEvents: false and userNotificationCenter(didReceive:withCompletionHandler:) is forwarded manually
column4: A non-CIO push is tapped
column5: The SDK returns false and does not call the completion handler; the caller must call it

column3: APNs token registration fails
column4: didFailToRegisterForRemoteNotifications(withError:) is called
column5: The SDK logs the error at the configured log level; no crash occurs

column3: No App Group availability has been recorded in sdk_meta
column4: configure() runs with an accessible App Group
column5: The result is stored in sdk_meta and a device attribute update is uploaded

column3: sdk_meta records App Group as available
column4: configure() runs and the App Group container is still accessible
column5: No device attribute event is uploaded; the check is a two-boolean comparison only

column3: sdk_meta records App Group as available
column4: configure() runs and the App Group container is no longer accessible
column5: sdk_meta is updated to false a device attribute event is uploaded and a warning is logged

column3: A push token is registered and events are in the App Group queue
column4: reset() is called
column5: The push token is cleared from DeviceStore the App Group push-delivery directory is deleted and the Module receives ResetEvent
